MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-29-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Pacific Bell Telephone Company For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Denied in its entirety. While plaintiff A Ruiz Construction Co. & Assoc., Inc. contends that it is a third party beneficiary of the 1974 agreement, AT&T does not concede that issue. At this point, Ruiz Construction is permitted to pursue alternative theories of recovery and correctly cites Crowley v. Katleman (1994) 8 Cal.4th 666, 690-691 to that effect. AT&T fails to show that the open book account cause of action cannot be established. A book account can be established by a course of dealing between the parties. (Warda v. Schmidt (1956) 146 Cal.App.2d 234, 237.) There is a triable issue whether the course of dealing between the parties established an implied agreement that Ruiz Construction would maintain a book account for AT&T. Ruiz Construction sent periodic statements to AT&T (Plaintiff's Additional Facts 15 and 16 (Cotter Dec., pars. 3-4)) and AT&T never protested (Plaintiff's Additional Fact 24 (Ruiz Dec., 5:1-3).) This is sufficient to establish a right to sue on a book account. (Id. at pp. 237-238.) Because AT&T has not conceded that Ruiz Construction is an intended third party beneficiary of the 1974 contract, there is no basis to hold that the 1974 contract displaces Ruiz Construction's ability to recover on a quantum meruit theory. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk
